content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
February 28, 1990

Stella Mathews
LOS Angeles County Registrar-Voters
P.O. Box 30450
LOS Angeles, CA 92123

Re: April 10, 1990 Special
Election and June 5, 1990,
Combined Filing Deadlines
Our File No. I-90-132

Dear Ms. Mathews:

You have requested assistance concerning the reporting
requirements for candidates for the 26th Senate District who will
be on the ballot for a special runoff election and the state
primary election, both of which will be held on June 5, 1990.
This assistance is provided under the campaign provisions of the
Political Reform Act.¹

A special election has been called for Senate District 26
which has become vacant because of the resignation of Senator
Joseph Montoya. The primary election date is April 10, 1990, and
the runoff election date is June 5, 1990.

You have asked us to provide the filing deadlines and periods
covered by campaign disclosure statements required to be filed by
candidates who will be on the ballot June 5, 1990, as candidates
in the special runoff election as well as candidates for their
party nomination for election to the Assembly in the state primary
election.

The filing deadlines and periods covered by campaign
statements required to be filed by these candidates are set out in
detail in Attachment 1.

Please note that the contribution limitations in the Act
apply separately for a special election cycle, for a special
runoff election cycle, and for a fiscal year. That is, a
candidate may receive contributions from a contributor up to the
"fiscal year" maximum amount during the "special election cycle,"
additional contributions up to the maximum amount during the

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Stella Mathews
Page Two

"special runoff election cycle," and candidates involved in the
primary election may accept additional contributions up to the
maximum amount during the "fiscal year." (See Attachment 2 for
definitions of contribution cycles.) For example, a candidate who
has a campaign bank account to receive contributions in connection
with the special election and an account to receive contributions
in connection with the regular state primary election may receive
$1,000 from a "person" into the special election account, and, if
the candidate is successful, another $1,000 from the same
individual into the special election account during the special
runoff election cycle. In addition, the candidate may receive
$1,000 during the fiscal year from the same individual into the
account established for the primary election.

Candidates may also transfer contributions from their special
election/special runoff election campaign committee account into
their campaign account for the regular state primary election.
However, if they do so, contributors of the funds transferred
cannot make additional contributions to the candidate that, when
added to the amount transferred, exceed the "fiscal year" limits.
In addition, campaign funds may not be transferred from a
candidate's special election/special runoff election account into
the candidate's other campaign accounts if such transfers would
cause the total amount contributed by a single source to the non-
special election account(s) to exceed the maximum amount for the
fiscal year. Attachment 2 sets out the allowable contributions
which may be received by candidates being voted upon in the
special election.

Please refer to the FPPC Interim Information Manual on
Propositions 68 and 73 for detailed information concerning the
contribution limitations. Please refer to the FPPC Information
Manual On Campaign Disclosure Provisions of the Political Reform
Act for information concerning other campaign disclosure
provisions.

2         Because the regular state general election is in a new fiscal
year (July 1, 1990 - June 30, 1991), additional contributions may
be received.

     The same campaign bank account may be used for the regular
state primary and general elections.

Stella Mathews
Page Three

     If you have additional questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        Kathryn E. Donovan
                                        General Counsel
                                        [signature]
                                        By: Lynda Cassady
                                            Political Reform Consultant

Enclosures
